mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, depressing forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO falls >1% and RSAFS falls >2% over a 3-month window, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sharp drop in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader consumer demand contraction, negatively impacting S&P 500 earnings expectations over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not precede SPX drawdowns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the predictive hazard ratio drops below 1.0 in Cox regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate cash flows materialize.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansionary quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 210, persistent inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces prolonged monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when CPIAUCSL crosses 210, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in payroll employment and a contraction in real personal savings rates indicate wage-driven demand that overheats the economy, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/RSAFS signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when PAYEMS rises >150k and RSAFS falls >5%, with walk-forward accuracy dropping below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index signals improved capacity utilization and lower input costs, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day window.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not precede positive SPX returns during recovery periods, or the predictive correlation drops below 0.1 over a 4-year rolling window.
